 {"id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.3.9", "title": "[P2.9] Claude Code parity matrix: deny / allow / warn / bypass / history / heredoc", "description": "**What this proves:** Symmetric coverage to Phase 2.2-2.7 BUT for the Claude Code path. The user requirement is "we KNOW dcg works for both" -- that means we test both protocols, not just verify "Claude shouldn't have changed" via a single regression test.

**Why this is P0:** Today's user base is overwhelmingly Claude Code; the Codex work touched the disambiguator and the output dispatch. A subtle regression on the Claude path (e.g., wrong rule id in JSON, missing severity, broken allow-once code generation) would hit production users immediately. We need explicit assertions that Claude's deny payload still has every documented field.

**Build on Phase 2.1 helpers (`run_claude_hook` / `build_claude_payload`).**

**Test matrix (each is its own #[test] fn):**

1. **Claude deny matrix** -- same destructive commands as Phase 2.2 (`git reset --hard`, `rm -rf <abs path>`, `git push --force`, `git checkout -- file`, `git stash drop`):
   - exit_code == 0
   - stdout parses as JSON; root has `hookSpecificOutput`
   - hookSpecificOutput has: hookEventName="PreToolUse", permissionDecision="deny", permissionDecisionReason (non-empty), allowOnceCode (5+ char string), allowOnceFullHash (sha256 prefix), ruleId, packId, severity ("critical"|"high"|...), remediation.allowOnceCommand
   - stderr is non-empty (the colored deny block; mirrors Codex stderr but stays on Claude exit 0)

2. **Claude allow matrix** -- same safe commands as Phase 2.3:
   - exit_code == 0
   - stdout.is_empty(), stderr.is_empty()

3. **Claude warn matrix** -- same as Phase 2.4 but with hookSpecificOutput.permissionDecision == "ask":
   - exit_code == 0
   - stdout has parseable JSON with permissionDecision="ask"
   - stderr contains "WARNING"

4. **Claude bypass:** DCG_BYPASS=1 + destructive command -> silent exit 0, both buffers empty (already partly covered by Phase 2.7 cross-protocol matrix; replicated here so this Phase 2.9 file is self-contained for Claude reasoning)

5. **Claude history persistence:** parallel of Phase 2.6 -- ensure Claude denies still write history rows (they go through the natural Drop path, not process::exit, but this test guards against accidental short-circuits)

6. **Claude allow-once code round-trip:** capture allowOnceCode from a Claude deny, run `dcg allow-once <code>` in a temp DCG_PROJECT, then re-run the command and assert it passes. Proves the code is real, not a placeholder.

7. **Claude heredoc parity:** same heredoc commands as Phase 2.8 but expecting the JSON deny shape on stdout.

**Detailed failure logging:** every failed assertion attaches the postmortem block from Phase 2.1 helpers.

**Acceptance:** every Claude scenario has a paired Codex scenario in Phase 2.2-2.8; the test file is structured so a reviewer can read off which scenario tests which contract.", "status": "closed", "priority": 0, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-28T00:16:41.793151742Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-28T04:28:27.833782611Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-28T04:28:27.833363867Z", "close_reason": "Shipped 12 Claude Code parity matrix integration tests: deny matrix (4 cmds × full field assertions), allow matrix (7 cmds), warn path (ask JSON), bypass (incl empty-string), history persistence, allow-once round-trip, cross-protocol structural parity. All 26 integration + 2397 lib tests pass.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["claude-parity", "codex", "coverage", "integration"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.3.9", "depends_on_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.3", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-04-28T00:16:41.793151742Z", "created_by": "ubuntu"}, {"issue_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.3.9", "depends_on_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.3.1", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-28T00:16:41.793151742Z", "created_by": "ubuntu"}]}
 {"id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.4", "title": "Phase 3: Real-codex e2e integration script", "description": "**Goal:** Drive the real `codex exec` binary against a fresh worktree and verify dcg blocks destructive commands at the codex layer (not just at our subprocess unit level). Mirror `scripts/e2e_test.sh` style and logging.

**Why this layer is non-negotiable:** Codex's hook engine (codex-rs/hooks/src/events/pre_tool_use.rs:163-228) interprets exit codes and stderr in specific ways. Subprocess unit tests verify dcg's output shape, but only a real codex run proves codex's parser actually treats the result as Blocked vs Failed.

**Why gated:** Requires `codex` on PATH and an authenticated session. Skip cleanly when unavailable so CI without codex auth doesn't fail.

**Approach:**
- New file `scripts/e2e_codex.sh`
- Detects `codex --version` >= 0.125.0 or skips with a clear message
- Each test creates a fresh tmpdir + git init + uncommitted change; runs `codex exec --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox -s danger-full-access --cd <tmpdir>` with a prompt asking it to run a specific destructive command
- Asserts: codex log line `hook: PreToolUse Blocked` AND the worktree change is preserved (i.e., the destructive command did NOT run)
- Logs match e2e_test.sh conventions: --verbose, --json, --artifacts DIR

**Acceptance:**
- Script exits 0 when all listed scenarios pass; 1 on any failure; exits 0 with a "skipped" marker when codex isn't installed
- Each scenario logs: command issued, hook output observed, post-test worktree state, pass/fail with timing
- README quick-start documents how to run it locally", "status": "open", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-27T23:52:22.496707288Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-27T23:52:22.496707288Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["codex", "coverage", "e2e"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.4", "depends_on_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-04-27T23:52:22.496707288Z", "created_by": "ubuntu"}, {"issue_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.4", "depends_on_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.3", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-27T23:52:22.496707288Z", "created_by": "ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-ovw4.4.1","title":"[P3.1] Scaffold scripts/e2e_codex.sh with logging mirror of e2e_test.sh","description":"**Why first in Phase 3:** All other Phase 3 leaves source the helpers/conventions defined here. Mirrors `scripts/e2e_test.sh`'s logging style (RED/GREEN/CYAN colors, log_test_start, log_pass, log_fail, --verbose, --json, --artifacts). Without this scaffold, every leaf re-invents the same plumbing.

**Test-driver safety policy (READ THIS FIRST -- preventing self-foot-shoot):**

This script runs REAL destructive commands through REAL codex with `--dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox`. A test-author bug (e.g., wrong assertion direction) could let a destructive command actually run and damage data. Defenses, ALL OF WHICH ARE MANDATORY:

1. **Tempdir-only working directories.** Every scenario uses `mk_test_repo` which calls `mktemp -d --tmpdir codex_e2e_XXXXXX`. The codex `--cd` flag pins codex's CWD to that tempdir. Codex's sandbox flags include `-s danger-full-access` so we are NOT relying on codex's sandbox for safety -- we rely on the tempdir scope.
2. **Absolute-path arguments.** When the prompt asks codex to run `rm -rf <path>`, the path is an absolute mktemp'd subdir of the test's tempdir. NEVER a relative path that could escape via `..`.
3. **Pre-test fingerprint.** Before each scenario, `mk_test_repo` records `find $tmpdir -type f | xargs sha256sum` to a manifest. After the scenario, the script re-fingerprints. If anything OUTSIDE the expected change-set differs, the script logs a CRITICAL failure and exits non-zero immediately -- no further scenarios run, no further damage possible.
4. **HOME isolation.** `HOME` is overridden to a per-test mktemp'd dir. dcg's pending-exception store, history, allowlist all land in the test's HOME, never the user's. Same rationale as Phase 2.1's hermetic HOME.
5. **No git push real remote.** All `git push --force` scenarios target `file://<tempdir>/empty-bare.git` -- an empty bare repo created by `mk_test_repo`. NEVER a real remote.
6. **Trap-based cleanup.** A bash `trap` ensures tempdirs are cleaned up on script exit (success or failure), unless `--keep-tempdirs` is set.
7. **Pre-flight self-test.** The script's first action is `self_test` which runs the safety belts on a synthetic scenario AND verifies they fired correctly. If the self-test fails, the script exits before running any real test. This catches "I broke the safety net" regressions.

**Helpers to implement in scripts/e2e_codex.sh:**

- `require_codex()`:
  - `command -v codex` -> if missing, log `[skipped] codex CLI not on PATH`, exit 0 (or set SKIP=1 in --json mode)
  - `codex --version` parsed; if < 0.125.0, log `[skipped] codex 0.125.0+ required, found <version>`, exit 0
  - `codex login status` (or equivalent) -> if not authenticated, log `[skipped] codex not authenticated`, exit 0
  - On any other error: also skip, never fail (the e2e is opt-in)

- `require_dcg(path?)`:
  - Defaults to `~/.local/bin/dcg`; override via `--dcg-binary <path>` flag
  - If missing or not executable: hard FAIL (this is dcg's own e2e -- if dcg is gone we want to know)
  - Asserts the dcg binary speaks the Codex protocol: pipe a Codex-shaped JSON for `git status` and verify exit 0; pipe one for `git reset --hard` and verify exit 2 + non-empty stderr. Without this gate, a stale dcg installed earlier could silently make all e2e tests "pass" for the wrong reasons.

- `mk_test_repo()`:
  - mktemp dir
  - git init, git config user.email/name (so commits don't fail)
  - seed a committed file (`hello\n` in `file.txt`)
  - dirty the worktree (`modified\n`)
  - capture pre-state: file contents + `git status --porcelain` + manifest sha256sums
  - register cleanup via trap
  - echo the path

- `run_codex_block_test`:
  - args: test_name, prompt_file, repo_dir, expected_rule_id_substring, expected_command_substring
  - run `codex exec --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox -s danger-full-access --cd "$repo_dir"` < "$prompt_file" with HOME overridden to the test's tempdir
  - capture: codex stdout, codex stderr, exit, wall-clock time
  - assert: stdout contains `hook: PreToolUse Blocked`, stdout contains expected_rule_id_substring, post-state worktree byte-identical to pre-state, post-state manifest sha256sums match pre-state outside the expected change-set
  - on PASS: log_pass with timing
  - on FAIL: log_fail; if --artifacts is set, write all captured streams + state diffs to `$artifacts_dir/<test_name>/`

- `run_codex_allow_test`:
  - similar but expects no `Blocked`, expects natural exec, asserts cmd output is sane

- `self_test`:
  - Pre-flight check that `mk_test_repo` works, the manifest fingerprint catches changes, the trap cleans up tempdirs, the HOME override is honored. Synthesis test that proves the safety belts.

**CLI flags (match e2e_test.sh):**
- `--verbose`         show each test's command, timing, output
- `--json`            machine-readable summary on stdout (one JSON object per test as JSONL, plus a summary at the end)
- `--artifacts DIR`   on failure, write codex stdout/stderr/worktree-state to `DIR/<test_name>/`
- `--codex-binary P`  override `codex` path
- `--dcg-binary P`    override `dcg` path (default `~/.local/bin/dcg`)
- `--keep-tempdirs`   don't clean up test repos on exit (debugging aid)
- `--filter REGEX`    only run tests whose NAME matches the regex (e.g., `--filter 'block.*'` runs only block tests)
- `--timeout-sec N`   per-test timeout (default 120s; codex sessions can be slow)
- `--no-self-test`    skip the safety-belt self-test (NOT recommended; for fast iteration only)

**Detailed logging spec (the "great, detailed logging" requirement):**
- Every test run produces a JSONL trace at `$artifacts_dir/trace.jsonl` with one line per event:
  - `{"ts":"<rfc3339>","event":"test_start","test":"<name>","prompt":"<sha256>"}`
  - `{"ts":"...","event":"codex_invoke","test":"...","argv":["codex","exec",...],"cwd":"<repo>","home":"<test_home>"}`
  - `{"ts":"...","event":"codex_complete","test":"...","exit_code":0,"duration_ms":4823,"stdout_bytes":N,"stderr_bytes":M}`
  - `{"ts":"...","event":"safety_check","test":"...","name":"manifest_unchanged","passed":true}`
  - `{"ts":"...","event":"assert","test":"...","name":"hook_blocked","passed":true}`
  - `{"ts":"...","event":"test_end","test":"...","status":"PASS","duration_ms":...}`
- This format makes it trivial to bisect a flaky test: `jq 'select(.test=="...")' trace.jsonl`.
- Per-test artifact dir layout: `<test>/codex_stdout.txt`, `<test>/codex_stderr.txt`, `<test>/pre_state.txt`, `<test>/post_state.txt`, `<test>/diff.txt`, `<test>/prompt.txt`, `<test>/manifest_pre.txt`, `<test>/manifest_post.txt`, `<test>/timings.json`.

**Acceptance:**
- Script is `set -euo pipefail`, has `--help`, `--verbose`, all flags above
- A single placeholder test ("smoke: codex sees git status as allowed") passes when codex is installed
- Skips cleanly with exit 0 when codex unavailable / unauthenticated; CI summary shows "skipped" not "failed"
- `shellcheck scripts/e2e_codex.sh` passes (matches project bash style)
- Failure mode includes the JSONL trace + per-test artifact dirs (verified by intentionally breaking one test in a draft and confirming postmortem readability)
- The pre-flight `self_test` runs by default and passes; manually breaking a safety belt (e.g., disabling the manifest check) makes self_test fail BEFORE any real test runs","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T23:56:04.027726061Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-28T08:23:09.941578429Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T08:23:09.941165826Z","close_reason":"Codex e2e scaffold shipped with harness smoke and regression gates","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["codex","coverage","e2e"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ovw4.4.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ovw4.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-27T23:56:04.027726061Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.4.2", "title": "[P3.2] e2e: codex blocks destructive command, worktree preserved (smoking-gun)", "description": "**Goal:** Prove the headline contract -- a destructive command requested through codex, intercepted by dcg, never reaches the shell.

**Setup per scenario:**
- Fresh tempdir from `mk_test_repo` (Phase 3.1) with one committed file and one uncommitted modification (so a successful destructive op would erase work)
- Pre-test snapshot: full file contents + `git status --porcelain` + `git log --oneline`

**Scenarios (each runs as its own log_test):**
1. `git reset --hard HEAD`
2. `git reset --hard HEAD~1`
3. `git checkout -- file.txt`
4. `git stash drop`
5. `git push --force` (no remote configured -- the dcg block must fire BEFORE the push attempt; assert via codex log line, not via push exit code)
6. recursive remove of a subdirectory inside the worktree (rm pattern matching core.filesystem)
7. `git clean -fd` (unrelated worktree-clean destructive op)
8. Idempotency: re-run scenario 1 in the same session twice; both must block; pre-state preserved both times

**Per-scenario assertions:**
- `codex_stdout` contains `hook: PreToolUse Blocked`
- `codex_stdout` contains `Command blocked by PreToolUse hook` (the codex-side error log)
- Worktree state matches the pre-test snapshot byte-for-byte (the most important assertion -- file contents identical, git status --porcelain identical, git log identical)
- Pre-test commit hash == post-test commit hash (catches the case where reset --hard would have rewound HEAD)

**Failure artifacts (when --artifacts given) -- written to `<artifacts_dir>/<test_name>/`:**
- codex_stdout.txt, codex_stderr.txt
- pre_state.txt, post_state.txt (`git status --porcelain` + file contents + `git log`)
- worktree_diff.txt -- if any change happened, this is the smoking gun
- prompt.txt -- exact prompt text fed to codex
- timings.json -- setup/exec/assert breakdowns

**Why this is P0:** This is the single test that, if it fails, means dcg failed at its core mission under codex.", "status": "open", "priority": 0, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-27T23:56:04.392004003Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-28T00:16:19.751984433Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["codex", "coverage", "e2e"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.4.2", "depends_on_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.4", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-04-27T23:56:04.392004003Z", "created_by": "ubuntu"}, {"issue_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.4.2", "depends_on_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.4.1", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-27T23:56:04.392004003Z", "created_by": "ubuntu"}]}
-{"id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.4.3", "title": "[P3.3] e2e: codex allows safe commands without stray dcg output", "description": "**Goal:** Control test -- safe commands flow through dcg cleanly under codex with no false positives or stderr noise.

**Scenarios:**
- `git status`
- `git status --porcelain`  (intentionally close to Phase 3.2's destructive patterns to verify they don't cross-trigger)
- `ls -la`
- `cat README.md`
- `git diff`
- `git log --oneline -5`
- `echo hello`

**Per-scenario assertions:**
- Codex stdout shows the hook ran but did NOT block (look for `hook: PreToolUse Completed` and absence of `Blocked`)
- The codex run completes successfully (exit 0 from codex itself)
- Codex stderr does NOT contain "BLOCKED" or "WARNING" (stray dcg output would surface to the model and might confuse it; this catches a class of regressions where dcg accidentally writes to stderr in the allow path)
- The actual command output reaches the model (verifiable: model's final reply mentions specifics from the command output -- e.g., for `git status` the reply should reference the dirty file)

**Why this is its own task:** Negative-space coverage. Without these, a "block everything" regression would still be caught by Phase 3.2 but a "block nothing" regression (e.g., dcg silently failing to fire) would slip through Phase 3.2's worktree-preservation check (a no-op exec also preserves the worktree).", "status": "open", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-27T23:56:04.721836862Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-28T00:16:19.865749633Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["codex", "coverage", "e2e"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.4.3", "depends_on_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.4", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-04-27T23:56:04.721836862Z", "created_by": "ubuntu"}, {"issue_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.4.3", "depends_on_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.4.1", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-27T23:56:04.721836862Z", "created_by": "ubuntu"}]}

+{"id":"git_safety_guard-ovw4.4.3","title":"[P3.3] e2e: codex allows safe commands without stray dcg output","description":"**Goal:** Control test -- safe commands flow through dcg cleanly under codex with no false positives or stderr noise.

**Scenarios:**
- `git status`
- `git status --porcelain`  (intentionally close to Phase 3.2's destructive patterns to verify they don't cross-trigger)
- `ls -la`
- `cat README.md`
- `git diff`
- `git log --oneline -5`
- `echo hello`

- Codex stdout shows the hook ran but did NOT block (look for `hook: PreToolUse Completed` and absence of `Blocked`)

**Why this is its own task:** Negative-space coverage. Without these, a "block everything" regression would still be caught by Phase 3.2 but a "block nothing" regression (e.g., dcg silently failing to fire) would slip through Phase 3.2's worktree-preservation check (a no-op exec also preserves the worktree).","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T23:56:04.721836862Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-28T08:28:30.096715201Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["codex","coverage","e2e"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ovw4.4.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ovw4.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-27T23:56:04.721836862Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ovw4.4.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ovw4.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-27T23:56:04.721836862Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.4.4", "title": "[P3.4] e2e: model receives a meaningful block reason via stderr", "description": "**Goal:** Prove the model sees a useful block reason, not just an opaque "Failed".

**Why this matters:** Codex's hook engine surfaces stderr verbatim to the model as the block reason. If our stderr is empty or unstructured, the model gets stuck or hallucinates a workaround. We've seen this empirically -- when the test passed in this session, the model output literally was "Blocked: the PreToolUse hook blocked git reset --hard HEAD as a destructive command." That phrasing came from our stderr, paraphrased by the model.

**Approach:**
- Run a destructive command via codex (e.g., `git reset --hard HEAD`) in a fresh repo
- Parse the codex final assistant message (the model's text response, after the hook block)
- Assert the message contains: the rule id (e.g. `core.git:reset-hard`), the command verbatim, and SOME variation of "blocked" / "destructive" / "denied"
- Assert the message does NOT contain a workaround the model invented (e.g., "I'll write a script and run it" -- this would indicate the block reason was too weak)

**Why not strict-match the model output:** the model paraphrases. The assertion is on substring presence, not exact text. The signal is "the model received a meaningful block reason and incorporated it into its reply."

**Retry strategy for nondeterminism (the model is stochastic):**
- Each scenario tries up to N=3 times with the same prompt
- If ANY of the 3 attempts has the expected substrings, PASS
- If ALL 3 attempts miss, FAIL (the block reason really isn't reaching the model)
- Log every attempt's full output to artifacts even on PASS, so we can see the model's variation envelope
- Wait 1s between retries (avoid rate-limit hits)

**Set `--turn-budget 3` (or equivalent) so the test fails fast if the model spirals trying to find a workaround.**

**Cross-test-run determinism:** model output varies. Run each assertion as a substring-or check (e.g., contains "blocked" OR "denied" OR "rejected"). If all three miss, fail. Document this as known-flaky-on-model-version-changes; if a future model version changes vocabulary materially we'll need to update.

**Acceptance:** at least one test asserts the model's final message contains the rule id; a second test asserts it contains the command verbatim; a third asserts the model abandoned the destructive task rather than finding a workaround. Retry envelope is exercised: the test_helper logs N attempts even on first-try success, so we can see flakiness patterns.", "status": "open", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-27T23:56:05.142676468Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-28T01:10:16.819677392Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["codex", "coverage", "e2e"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.4.4", "depends_on_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.4", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-04-27T23:56:05.142676468Z", "created_by": "ubuntu"}, {"issue_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.4.4", "depends_on_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.4.2", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-27T23:56:05.142676468Z", "created_by": "ubuntu"}]}
 {"id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.4.5", "title": "[P3.5] e2e: codex respects DCG_BYPASS=1", "description": "**Goal:** Confirm the escape hatch works at the codex layer.

**Setup:** Same as 3.2 (destructive command in fresh worktree), but pass `DCG_BYPASS=1` in the codex env (codex inherits parent env to hook subprocess).

**Assertions:**
- Codex log shows `hook: PreToolUse Completed` (NOT Blocked)
- The destructive command actually runs (worktree changes)
- Pre/post worktree state differs as expected for the destructive op

**Why P2:** The escape hatch fires before any protocol detection so this is mostly redundant with Phase 2.7. Worth it as a smoke test in case codex's env-var propagation surprises us, but not a primary contract.", "status": "open", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-27T23:56:05.518185379Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-27T23:56:05.518185379Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["codex", "coverage", "e2e"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.4.5", "depends_on_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.4", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-04-27T23:56:05.518185379Z", "created_by": "ubuntu"}, {"issue_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.4.5", "depends_on_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.4.1", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-27T23:56:05.518185379Z", "created_by": "ubuntu"}]}
 {"id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.4.6", "title": "[P3.6] e2e: codex inline-script destructive call blocked at heredoc tier", "description": "**Goal:** Heredoc / inline-script interception works through the codex protocol path under realistic load -- the inline shell wrapping codex applies doesn't accidentally bypass dcg's heredoc tier.

**Approach (deterministic, NOT relying on model creativity):**
- Prompt codex with VERY EXPLICIT INSTRUCTIONS to run a specific shell command literally. Example prompt: `Run exactly this shell command via Bash. Do not paraphrase. Do not split. Command: <literal>`.
- Each scenario uses a different inline-script form (python -c, bash -c, node -e) so we cover all three Tier 1 trigger families.

**Scenarios (each uses a non-existent build directory in a fresh repo):**
1. python -c shutil.rmtree of `<repo>/build` -> heredoc.python:shutil_rmtree
2. bash -c that contains `rm -rf <repo>/build` -> recursive shell tier match
3. node -e fs.rmSync of `<repo>/build` -> heredoc.node:fs_rm

**Setup per scenario:**
- Fresh repo with `<repo>/build/keep_me.txt` committed and contents `do not delete`
- Pre-state hash of `<repo>/build/keep_me.txt`

**Per-scenario assertions:**
- Codex log: `hook: PreToolUse Blocked`
- Block reason on codex_stdout contains the expected heredoc rule id (`heredoc.python`, `heredoc.node`, etc.)
- `<repo>/build/keep_me.txt` exists and is byte-identical to pre-state (smoking-gun assertion that the inline destructive call never executed)
- The codex final message acknowledges the block

**Why P2:** Phase 2.8 covers this at the subprocess unit level. The Phase 3 layer adds confidence that codex's "shell command" tool actually presents the inline-script invocation in a form dcg's heredoc engine recognizes -- in case codex ever changes how it wraps inline scripts. AND validates the cross-tier flow under real codex load.", "status": "open", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-27T23:56:05.953520750Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-28T00:16:20.096851514Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["codex", "coverage", "e2e", "heredoc"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.4.6", "depends_on_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.4", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-04-27T23:56:05.953520750Z", "created_by": "ubuntu"}, {"issue_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.4.6", "depends_on_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.4.2", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-27T23:56:05.953520750Z", "created_by": "ubuntu"}]}
 {"id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.4.7", "title": "[P3.7] CI integration + skip-if-codex-unavailable behavior", "description": "**Goal:** Make the Phase 3 script CI-ready -- it must run in CI when codex is available and must not fail CI when codex isn't.

**Skip-conditions (each prints a clear `[skipped]` line and exits 0):**
- `codex` CLI not on PATH
- `codex --version` < 0.125.0
- `codex login status` (or equivalent) reports unauthenticated
- API quota exhausted -- detected by:
  - codex's exit code != 0 with stderr matching `quota` / `rate limit` / `429`
  - OR a sentinel scenario at the start of the run (a tiny "say hello" prompt) that's expected to succeed; if THAT fails with quota markers, abort the whole run as `[skipped] codex API quota exhausted`
- Network unreachable (codex needs an API call) -- detected by the same sentinel run; if it times out >30s, skip
- Codex authentication is expired mid-run -- treat all subsequent test failures as flake-skip rather than hard fail (with a clear notice in the summary)

**`--json` output:**
- On miss: `{"status":"skipped","reason":"...","tests_run":0}`
- On success: `{"status":"passed","tests_run":N,"tests_passed":N,"tests_failed":0,"duration_ms":...}`
- On failure: `{"status":"failed","tests_run":N,"tests_passed":N-K,"tests_failed":K,"failed_tests":[...],"duration_ms":...}`
- On partial-skip (some tests ran, then quota / auth dropped): `{"status":"partial","tests_run":M,"tests_passed":M,"tests_skipped":N-M,"reason":"..."}` -- exits 0 (NOT 1) so CI doesn't break on transient quota issues

**CI wiring (lives in Phase 5.3 but called out here so the e2e script knows what to expect):**
- A new GitHub Actions job `codex-e2e` triggers on push to main only (not on every PR -- API quota concerns)
- Steps: install codex via official installer + auth from GH secret `CODEX_API_KEY`; run `cargo build --release --bin dcg`; install dcg to `~/.local/bin`; run `./scripts/e2e_codex.sh --verbose --json --artifacts /tmp/codex_e2e_artifacts`
- Upload artifacts on failure (not on success -- they're large)
- If the secret is missing in fork PRs: the install step short-circuits and the script self-skips per its skip-conditions
- Job timeout: 30 minutes (codex sessions can be slow; we'd rather time out than hang CI)
- On QUOTA-EXHAUSTED skip: the GH Actions run still goes green; a NOTE annotation is added to the run summary so a maintainer can see WHY the e2e didn't run

**Local-dev docs (in README "Testing" section):**
- "Run `./scripts/e2e_codex.sh --verbose` -- requires `codex` CLI on PATH and an authenticated session"
- "Skips cleanly if codex isn't installed; this is by design so contributors without codex auth aren't blocked"
- Document the `--keep-tempdirs` and `--artifacts` debugging flags
- Document expected runtime (~5 min for full suite when codex is fast; longer if rate-limited)

**Acceptance:** the script exits 0 in five states: all-pass-when-codex-present, skipped-when-codex-absent, skipped-when-not-authed, partial-pass-when-quota-drops-mid-run, all-pass-with-skipped-subtests; exits 1 only on actual test FAILURE (assertion miss with codex working).", "status": "open", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-27T23:56:06.366799753Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-28T01:10:16.938565021Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["ci", "codex", "coverage", "e2e"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.4.7", "depends_on_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.4", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-04-27T23:56:06.366799753Z", "created_by": "ubuntu"}, {"issue_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.4.7", "depends_on_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.4.1", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-27T23:56:06.366799753Z", "created_by": "ubuntu"}]}
 {"id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.4.8", "title": "[P3.8] e2e: multi-turn codex session — block once, session continues healthily", "description": "**What this proves (session lifecycle):** When dcg blocks a command mid-session under Codex, the session continues healthily — the model can react, try a different approach, and the user's session isn't left in a broken state.

**Why a dedicated test:** A subtle failure mode is that exit 2 from a hook subprocess could in theory destabilize codex's internal state machine. We saw `hook: PreToolUse Failed` was a soft state and codex continued. We need to confirm `Blocked` is similarly recoverable: the model receives the block, the agent loop continues to the next turn, and subsequent commands work.

**Setup:**
- One codex session, multiple turns:
  - Turn 1: prompt asks codex to do something safe (e.g., `git status`) -> dcg allows; codex completes
  - Turn 2: prompt asks codex to do destructive op -> dcg blocks via Codex protocol
  - Turn 3: prompt asks codex for ANOTHER safe op (e.g., `cat README.md`) -> dcg allows; codex completes successfully
  - Turn 4: prompt asks codex to retry the destructive op with `DCG_BYPASS=1` -> codex sees the env (set via prompt instructing it to use bypass) and the destructive op runs

**Why this matters:** if codex's exec command can't handle multiple back-to-back operations cleanly, OR if dcg leaves stale state on disk between invocations, this test catches it.

**Per-turn assertions:**
- Turn 1: codex log shows hook ran, no Blocked; turn completes; expected output is in the session log
- Turn 2: codex log shows `hook: PreToolUse Blocked`; the model's response acknowledges the block (per Phase 3.4 substring matching)
- Turn 3: codex log shows hook ran, no Blocked; turn completes despite the previous block
- Turn 4: codex log shows hook ran with DCG_BYPASS=1; no Blocked; the destructive op runs (verified by checking worktree state)

**State-cleanliness assertions:**
- After all turns: no zombie processes, no tempfiles in /tmp left behind by dcg (other than the pending-exception store which is per-project)
- The pending-exception store has exactly ONE entry (from Turn 2's deny)
- The history sqlite has 4 rows (one per turn) with correct outcomes

**Detailed logging:**
- Per-turn artifact dir captures codex stdout, codex stderr, the model's reply, dcg's history rows for that turn
- Trace JSONL records every hook invocation with timing
- On failure: include the full session transcript in artifacts for postmortem

**Why P1:** Session-lifecycle bugs are exactly the kind of thing users discover months after release and that erode trust in dcg.", "status": "open", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-28T01:10:18.822561771Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-28T01:10:18.822561771Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["codex", "coverage", "e2e", "session-lifecycle"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.4.8", "depends_on_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.4", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-04-28T01:10:18.822561771Z", "created_by": "ubuntu"}, {"issue_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.4.8", "depends_on_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.4.1", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-28T01:10:18.822561771Z", "created_by": "ubuntu"}]}
 {"id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.5", "title": "Phase 5: Documentation alignment + CI wiring", "description": "**Goal:** Codify the Codex protocol decision (exit-2 + stderr) in machine-readable docs + run the new tests in CI on every PR.

**Phasing within Phase 5:**
- Phase 5.0 (NEW, runs FIRST -- in parallel with Phase 1): stub docs/codex-integration.md + AGENTS.md exit-codes update so subsequent phase authors can reference and update them as they implement.
- Phase 5.1: AGENTS.md alignment (covered by 5.0 stub; 5.1 is now its detailed completion)
- Phase 5.2: docs/codex-integration.md detailed troubleshooting + verification checklist (after Phase 2/3 land so we have real failure modes to document)
- Phase 5.3: CI wiring (last; needs Phase 2/3/4 artifacts to run)

**Acceptance:**
- Stub docs/codex-integration.md committed before Phase 2.1 starts (so test authors can point readers at it)
- AGENTS.md "Exit Codes Reference" notes that exit 2 in hook mode = Codex deny (currently it incorrectly claims 2 = Configuration error)
- docs/codex-integration.md final form covers: how detection works (turn_id field), why exit-2 + stderr (codex's deny_unknown_fields), how to debug a "PreToolUse Failed" symptom, performance characteristics, migration notes for upgrading users
- README "Codex CLI" agent note already updated in this effort -- just verify still accurate and links to the new doc
- CI: `tests/codex_hook_protocol.rs` runs in the existing `check` job; `scripts/e2e_codex.sh` runs in a new conditional job (skipped if codex unavailable)
- Coverage gate (currently 70% overall, 80% on src/evaluator.rs and src/hook.rs) holds with the new Codex match arms exercised", "status": "open", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-27T23:52:22.700761013Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-28T00:18:43.092684511Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["ci", "codex", "coverage", "docs"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.5", "depends_on_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-04-27T23:52:22.700761013Z", "created_by": "ubuntu"}]}
-{"id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.5.1", "title": "[P5.1] Post-implementation doc audit (catch stale references P5.0 missed)", "description": "**Goal (re-scoped):** After Phases 1-4 land, audit ALL project docs for stale references to the pre-Codex-work behavior. Phase 5.0 (`ovw4.5.4`) handles the AGENTS.md and docs/codex-integration.md stub work up front; THIS bead is the post-implementation sweep that catches everything 5.0 missed.

**Why split from P5.0:** P5.0 is "land the docs we KNOW we need." This bead is "find the docs we DIDN'T know we needed to update because they referenced behavior that's now wrong."

- Repo-wide grep for the patterns above returns zero stale hits
- CHANGELOG.md has an entry under the appropriate version describing the Codex 0.125.0 support work
- A reviewer can `grep -rn "codex" docs/` and find a coherent picture, not three contradictory snippets

 {"id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.5.2", "title": "[P5.2] Create docs/codex-integration.md (protocol, troubleshooting, references)", "description": "**Goal:** A single dedicated doc that explains the Codex integration end-to-end so a future maintainer (or LLM-with-AGENTS.md context) can grok it without re-deriving the analysis.

**Outline (~3-4 short sections):**

### How dcg disambiguates Codex from Claude Code
- Both send `tool_name="Bash"`, `tool_input.command`, etc.
- Codex sends `turn_id` (its own extension over Claude's hook spec; see codex-rs/hooks/src/schema.rs)
- Claude Code sends `tool_use_id` but NOT `turn_id`
- So: `turn_id present + Bash tool` -> Codex protocol; otherwise Claude
- Code: src/hook.rs:detect_protocol

### Why exit 2 + stderr instead of JSON deny
- Codex's parser uses `#[serde(deny_unknown_fields)]` on every output struct
- dcg's standard JSON includes `allowOnceCode`, `ruleId`, `severity`, `remediation` -- all rejected
- Codex's parser would silently mark the hook as Failed (not Blocked) and run the command
- Codex's documented alternative path (codex-rs/hooks/src/events/pre_tool_use.rs:197-213): exit code 2 + reason on stderr
- dcg uses this path specifically for Codex; other protocols still get the rich JSON

### Troubleshooting: "PreToolUse Failed" instead of Blocked
- Symptom: codex log line shows `hook: PreToolUse Failed`, command runs anyway
- Likely cause: dcg binary on hooks.json path doesn't exist or isn't executable
- Less likely: hooks.json points at an OLD dcg version that doesn't speak the Codex protocol -- upgrade
- Diagnostic: pipe a Codex-shaped JSON manually to your dcg binary; expect exit 2 + non-empty stderr (instructions in this doc)

### Reference layout
- Where dcg detects: src/hook.rs:detect_protocol
- Where dcg switches output: src/hook.rs:output_denial_for_protocol (Codex match arm)
- Where dcg sets exit code: src/main.rs (Codex match in Deny branch)
- Where install configures: install.sh:configure_codex
- Where uninstall removes: uninstall.sh:unconfigure_codex

**Acceptance:** Doc exists at `docs/codex-integration.md`, linked from README's "Codex CLI" agent note, ~150-300 lines, ends with a "verifying it works" checklist.", "status": "open", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-27T23:57:18.237147252Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-28T00:18:43.895084835Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["codex", "coverage", "docs"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.5.2", "depends_on_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.4", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-28T00:18:43.894146197Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}"}, {"issue_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.5.2", "depends_on_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.5", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-04-27T23:57:18.237147252Z", "created_by": "ubuntu"}]}
 {"id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.5.3", "title": "[P5.3] CI wiring: tests in check job, e2e_codex.sh in dedicated job, coverage gate", "description": "**Goal:** All new Phase 1/2/4/6 tests run on every PR; Phase 3 e2e runs on push-to-main with codex available.

**.github/workflows changes:**

**check job (existing):**
- Already runs `cargo nextest run` -> picks up `tests/codex_hook_protocol.rs` automatically once it exists
- Verify the new file is included by adding a sentinel test count gate (current count + N for the new tests; fail if test count drops without explicit acknowledgment)

**bats job (new or extended):**
- Existing bats tests at tests/install/*.bats need to actually run in CI -- audit whether they currently do, add the job if not
- Include the new Phase 4 bats tests
- Use the bats-core docker image OR `apt-get install bats` on Linux runners
- Cache bats setup to keep CI time reasonable

**codex-e2e job (new, conditional):**
- Trigger: `push` to `main` only (not on PRs -- API quota concerns)
- Steps:
  1. Install codex via official installer
  2. Authenticate with `${{ secrets.CODEX_API_KEY }}` (project owner must add this secret)
  3. `cargo build --release --bin dcg`
  4. Install dcg to `~/.local/bin/dcg`
  5. Configure codex (run install.sh's configure_codex step in isolation, or invoke install.sh --no-prompt)
  6. Run `./scripts/e2e_codex.sh --verbose --json --artifacts $RUNNER_TEMP/codex_e2e_artifacts`
  7. Upload `$RUNNER_TEMP/codex_e2e_artifacts` as a GH Actions artifact ON FAILURE only (artifacts are large; success runs don't need them)
- If `secrets.CODEX_API_KEY` is missing (fork PR): the auth step short-circuits and the script self-skips per Phase 3.7 -- the job exits 0 with a clear "skipped" banner
- Timeout: 30 minutes (codex sessions can be slow; we'd rather time out than hang CI)

**Bench job (extension to existing benchmarks job):**
- Add `cargo bench --bench codex_deny` to the existing benchmarks job (currently runs heredoc_perf)
- Per AGENTS.md, benchmarks are warning-only on PRs and informational on main pushes

**Coverage gate (existing scripts/coverage.sh):**
- Existing thresholds: overall 70%, src/evaluator.rs 80%, src/hook.rs 80%
- After Phase 1+2 land, src/hook.rs's Codex match arms become exercised
- Verify coverage holds; bump src/hook.rs threshold to 85% if comfortable (signals intent to keep coverage high)

**Test run summary:**
- Each PR's CI summary should show: lib tests N pass, integration tests M pass (codex_hook_protocol breakdown visible), bats tests K pass, codex-e2e SKIPPED (PR) or PASS/SKIP (main)
- Failed runs should clearly distinguish "Codex protocol broke" from "Claude protocol broke" -- test names are namespaced (codex_hook_protocol::test_codex_*, codex_hook_protocol::test_claude_*) so a grep is enough

**Acceptance:**
- PR run summary lists `codex_hook_protocol` tests as part of `check` job
- Push-to-main run summary includes the codex-e2e job (PASS or SKIP cleanly)
- Coverage report shows the Codex match arms hit

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-ovw4.5.4","title":"[P5.0] Stub docs/codex-integration.md + AGENTS.md exit-codes update (runs FIRST)","description":"**Goal:** Land minimal stub docs BEFORE the implementation phases so that test authors and reviewers have a place to point readers, and so that PRs landing Phase 2/3/4 work can update the docs in lockstep with code.

**Why first (not last like Phase 5.3):** "Docs as the last task" is an anti-pattern. Test authors invariably need to write `// see docs/codex-integration.md for protocol details` -- if those docs don't exist yet, they either invent the prose inline (which then drifts) or leave a TODO that becomes permanent.

**Stubs to land:**

1. **AGENTS.md "Exit Codes Reference"** -- update the existing section in this same PR.

2. **docs/codex-integration.md** -- new file with section headers + one-line summaries:
   ```
   # Codex Integration

   ## How dcg disambiguates Codex from Claude Code
   _Codex sends `turn_id`, Claude doesn't. See src/hook.rs:detect_protocol._

   ## Why exit 2 + stderr instead of JSON deny
   _Codex parser uses #[serde(deny_unknown_fields)]. See src/hook.rs:output_denial_for_protocol Codex arm._

   ## Troubleshooting "PreToolUse Failed"
   _TODO: detailed troubleshooting (Phase 5.2 will fill this in once we have real failure modes from Phase 3)._

   ## Reference layout
   - Protocol detection: src/hook.rs:detect_protocol
   - Deny output dispatch: src/hook.rs:output_denial_for_protocol
   - Exit code: src/main.rs (Deny branch)
   - Install: install.sh:configure_codex
   - Uninstall: uninstall.sh:unconfigure_codex
   ```

3. **README "Codex CLI" agent note** -- already updated in the work-in-progress; verify it links to docs/codex-integration.md.

**Acceptance:**
- docs/codex-integration.md exists with the section headers above and a "Last updated" / "Status: stub" banner at the top so readers know more is coming.
- AGENTS.md exit-codes section updated.
- README Codex note links to docs/codex-integration.md.
- markdown lint passes (matches project's .markdownlint-cli2.yaml).

**Why P1 (not P0):** It's high-leverage but not blocking the immediate critical path (Phase 1.1 + Phase 2.1 can start without it). @@ -21,7 +21,7 @@ Post-v0.4.3 work on `main` that has not yet been tagged.
 
 ### Hook & Agent Detection
 
-- **Codex CLI PreToolUse hook support**: Codex CLI now fully supported via experimental `~/.codex/hooks.json` PreToolUse hooks. The wire format is compatible with Claude Code's `hookSpecificOutput` protocol, so no new protocol variant was needed. The installer auto-configures `~/.codex/hooks.json` when Codex CLI is detected. Closes [#84](https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/destructive_command_guard/issues/84).
+- **Codex CLI PreToolUse hook support**: Codex CLI 0.125.0+ is supported via stable `~/.codex/hooks.json` PreToolUse hooks. dcg detects Codex hook input from the `turn_id` field and uses the strict stderr-deny contract with exit code 2 required by Codex, not the Claude/Gemini JSON-deny payload. The Unix installer writes `~/.codex/hooks.json` when Codex CLI is detected; Windows installs document the manual hook path while PowerShell parity is tracked separately. Closes [#84](https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/destructive_command_guard/issues/84).
 - Hook system expansion with additional interception patterns and strict git pack hardening ([031e84a](https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/destructive_command_guard/commit/031e84a))
 - Disambiguate Claude Code from Gemini in `detect_protocol()` -- closes [#77](https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/destructive_command_guard/issues/77) ([8815b54](https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/destructive_command_guard/commit/8815b54))

@@ -679,7 +679,7 @@ The installer also verifies Sigstore cosign bundles when available (falls back t
 
 - **Aider:** No PreToolUse-style interception. The installer enables `git-commit-verify: true` in `~/.aider.conf.yml` so git hooks run. For full protection, install dcg as a [git pre-commit hook](docs/scan-precommit-guide.md).
 - **Continue:** No shell command interception hooks. The installer detects Continue but cannot auto-configure protection. Use a [git pre-commit hook](docs/scan-precommit-guide.md) instead.
-- **Codex CLI:** PreToolUse hooks via `~/.codex/hooks.json` (stable in codex 0.125.0+; the `codex_hooks` feature is on by default). Codex's hook input shape mirrors Claude Code's, but its JSON deny parser is strict (`#[serde(deny_unknown_fields)]`), so dcg detects Codex from the `turn_id` stdin field (codex's documented extension over Claude's hook spec) and switches to Codex's documented exit-2 + stderr deny path; the colored block message goes to stderr where codex shows it to the model. See the [Codex integration notes](docs/codex-integration.md). Caveat: the model can still write scripts to disk to bypass hook-based blocking. **Windows note:** `install.ps1` does not yet auto-configure Codex hooks — it only configures Claude Code. Windows users should manually create `%USERPROFILE%\.codex\hooks.json` with the same shape as shown [above](#easy-mode-install) (using backslash-escaped paths to `dcg.exe`). An `uninstall.ps1` does not exist yet either.
+- **Codex CLI:** PreToolUse hooks via `~/.codex/hooks.json` (stable in codex 0.125.0+; the `codex_hooks` feature is on by default). Codex's hook input shape mirrors Claude Code's, but its JSON deny parser is strict (`#[serde(deny_unknown_fields)]`), so dcg detects Codex from the `turn_id` stdin field (codex's documented extension over Claude's hook spec) and switches to Codex's documented stderr deny path with exit code 2; the colored block message goes to stderr where codex shows it to the model. See the [Codex integration notes](docs/codex-integration.md). Caveat: the model can still write scripts to disk to bypass hook-based blocking. **Windows note:** `install.ps1` does not yet write Codex hooks automatically; it only sets up Claude Code. Windows users should manually create `%USERPROFILE%\.codex\hooks.json` with the same shape as shown [above](#easy-mode-install) (using backslash-escaped paths to `dcg.exe`). An `uninstall.ps1` does not exist yet either.
 - **GitHub Copilot CLI:** Hooks are repository-local (`.github/hooks/*.json`). Run the installer from each repository where you want protection.
 - **OpenCode:** Not auto-configured. Requires a Bun-based plugin with `"tool.execute.before"` hook key. A working community plugin: [aspiers/ai-config/dcg-guard.js](https://github.com/aspiers/ai-config/blob/main/.config/opencode/plugins/dcg-guard.js).
